title: Branch Site Loses Internet After Router Maintenance

ticket.summary: The Reno branch office lost all internet access at 06:30 this morning, right after scheduled maintenance on the branch edge router br-reno-edge01. Internal LAN traffic and site-to-site VPN to HQ still work fine, but nothing reaches the public internet (no web browsing, no cloud SaaS access). Staff started arriving at 08:00 and immediately opened tickets.

topology.description: The Reno branch LAN (192.168.40.0/24) is served by edge router br-reno-edge01, which has a WAN uplink to ISP router isp-reno-gw (203.0.113.1) and a secondary interface toward the HQ VPN concentrator. During last night's maintenance window, the ISP circuit was reterminated and the ISP handed off a new gateway address, but the router's default route was not fully updated to match.

logs.content:
Jul 14 06:22:03 br-reno-edge01 CHANGE: user admin configured static route 0.0.0.0/0
Jul 14 06:22:03 br-reno-edge01 CHANGE: route committed, next-hop 203.0.113.254
Jul 14 06:22:04 br-reno-edge01 %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/1, changed state to up
Jul 14 06:30:11 br-reno-edge01 %ICMP-4-UNREACH: destination unreachable sending to 8.8.8.8 via 203.0.113.254
Jul 14 06:30:44 br-reno-edge01 %ICMP-4-UNREACH: destination unreachable sending to 1.1.1.1 via 203.0.113.254

08:02 user1: internet is down, can load internal timesheet app fine though
08:05 user2: same here, teams/zoom won't connect
08:07 it-onsite: rebooted my laptop, still nothing outbound. LAN stuff (fileserver, printer) works

!!!!!

Circuit ID: RNO-4471-FIBER

ISP gateway (new): 203.0.113.1
Effective: 2026-07-14 06:00 (post cutover)
Note: previous gateway 203.0.113.254 has been decommissioned as part of the fiber cutover.

monitoring.notes: PRTG shows the physical WAN interface on br-reno-edge01 as up/up continuously since 06:22, but the 'default gateway reachability' sensor (ICMP to configured next-hop) has been failing 100% since 06:30, immediately after the static route change. A separate sensor pinging the ISP's actual handoff IP (203.0.113.1) shows healthy round-trip times the entire time, indicating the physical circuit is fine and the problem is purely a routing configuration issue.

hints: LAN and site-to-site VPN traffic work, but internet doesn't — that isolates the problem to the WAN default route on br-reno-edge01.
Check whether the static default route's next-hop address is actually reachable from br-reno-edge01.
The default route points to 203.0.113.254, but the ISP's cutover sheet shows the new gateway is 203.0.113.1 — 203.0.113.254 was decommissioned during last night's fiber cutover.

solution.root_cause: During last night's maintenance the ISP re-terminated the circuit and reassigned the gateway address to 203.0.113.1, but br-reno-edge01's default static route was configured (or left) pointing to the old, now-decommissioned gateway 203.0.113.254, which is unreachable.
solution.fix: Update the default static route on br-reno-edge01 to use next-hop 203.0.113.1 instead of 203.0.113.254, matching the new ISP handoff.
